segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Opening theme - mysterious, building tension]
segments.direction: Ethereal, otherworldly feel establishing Aethon setting

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three things I know for certain: this job was always wrong, the patron who hired me was lying, and I'm about to find out why.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My name's Pixel Paradox. I hunt artifacts—Builder technology, mostly. Ancient things that still hum with Signal energy if you know where to look. I work alone, document everything, and keep meticulous records of every discovery. Independent work. My own documentation. No affiliations with the Chronicle, no guild contracts, no Rootminder oversight—just me and whatever mysteries Aethon wants to give up.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Usually, I'm better at saying no to suspicious jobs from anonymous patrons who pay too well.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [HOLOGRAM PROJECTION - gentle electronic hum, Arc's interface activating]

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, solar angle analysis indicates we have approximately four hours of optimal daylight remaining.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Noted.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I say, adjusting my pack. The weight feels good—familiar. Rope coiled just right, climbing picks secured, notebook in the waterproof pocket where I can grab it fast. Everything in its place. Everything ready.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Everything except the knot in my stomach that says this is a mistake.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'm camped at the edge of the coordinates—fifty meters from the temple entrance, far enough to observe, close enough to move fast if needed. My tent is a splash of weather-beaten orange against the overwhelming green of Verdanya's equatorial jungle. Three days' hike from the nearest settlement. Five days from anywhere with reliable Signal communication.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Alone. Exactly how I like it.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Except I keep thinking about the patron. The way the job appeared in my inbox with no introduction, no negotiation. Just coordinates, payment already deposited, and three words: Retrieve the device.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: No context. No explanation. No answer when I tried to trace the sender.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Just money. A lot of money. The kind that makes you forget to ask questions—like why a sacred Green Faith site suddenly has an 'approved outsider' exemption with my name on it. The Rootminder elder who met me at the forest's edge three days ago didn't ask questions either. Just pressed her palm to mine in greeting and said, 'Walk root-deep, artifact hunter. The jungle watches.'

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then she left me with a warning: 'The tall ones built that place before the Green took root. It remembers them still. Tread carefully.'

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc, pull up the site analysis again.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [HOLOGRAM PROJECTION - geometric patterns materializing, soft blue-white light]

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: My wristband projects a hologram—geometric patterns in blue-white light, nothing fancy. A three-dimensional map of the temple structure based on his ground-penetrating scans. It shouldn't exist. Not here, not this intact.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Verdanya doesn't preserve dead things. The jungle reclaims everything—stone, metal, even Builder technology eventually succumbs to the aggressive, voracious life that defines this region. Bio-luminescent vines consume structural weakness. Fungal colonies dissolve metal over decades. Trees split foundations with patient, inevitable roots.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But this temple stands. Eight centuries old, maybe older, and the jungle has only decorated it. Not consumed it.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Environmental scans continue to indicate structural instability. However, analysis also detects active Signal signatures within. Very faint, but present. The structure appears to be... maintained. By something.

audio_cues.tone: formal, measured, procedural with slight uncertainty

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Meaning you don't know.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Correct. I do not know. Proceeding with caution is advised.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [JUNGLE AMBIENCE - bird calls, insect drones, distant sounds of life]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The passage slopes downward at a gentle but steady angle. My boots echo on stone that hasn't heard footsteps in—what? Centuries? Longer? The walls are covered in those geometric patterns that mark Builder construction everywhere on Aethon, but these feel different. More deliberate. More complex.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I stop, running my fingers over the nearest pattern. The stone is cold, smooth, unmarked by time in ways that shouldn't be possible. The pattern is precise—mathematical precision, but there's something else. Intentionality. Like it's not just decoration.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Like it's meant to be read.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Up close, the carved lines look less organic, less artistic. More... technical. I trace one sequence with my fingertip and my brain keeps trying to make it make sense. Pressure gradients? Flow diagrams? The kind of thing you'd see in a Cuprum engineering manual, not on a sacred site's walls.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: One word catches my attention, repeated in the pattern: a symbol that almost looks like it could mean 'calibration.' Or maybe 'adjustment.' Definitely not 'prayer.'

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc, are you imaging these markings?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Affirmative. High-resolution scans being recorded. However, I have no reference database for translation. These patterns do not match any known Builder script in my archives.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I pull out my notebook, sketching quickly. The patterns flow in spirals, geometric precision creating organic curves. My pencil moves fast, capturing the essential structure before we move on. Unique pattern language. Not decorative—informational? Warning? Welcome? No way to know. Builders left no Rosetta Stone. But if this IS an engineering facility disguised as a temple... who were they trying to hide it from? Their own people? Or ours?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The passage continues. Deeper. Darker despite the bioluminescence. Colder too, which shouldn't be possible this close to the equator.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then I see it.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The corridor ends in a doorway—or what should be a doorway. Instead, there's a wall of that same black stone, but this wall is covered in a pattern more intricate than anything I've seen before. Concentric circles, geometric shapes, mathematical precision creating something that looks almost organic. Almost alive.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And in the center: an absence. A handprint-shaped depression.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc, analysis?

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [SCANNING HUM - Arc's sensor engagement, geometric processing sounds]

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Scanning. The pattern appears to be... a lock. Primitive by modern standards, but elegant. The depression in the center is sized for humanoid contact. I believe this is a puzzle lock—solution-based entry rather than key-based.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I step closer, studying the pattern. The circles radiate outward from the central depression. Each circle is divided into segments, and each segment contains smaller geometric shapes. Triangles, squares, pentagons, hexagons.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mathematical progression. The pattern of prime numbers, maybe? Or Fibonacci sequences?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Builders thought in mathematics, in pure geometric precision. Their locks weren't about keeping people out—they were about ensuring only those who understood could enter.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: A test of comprehension, not possession.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc, starting from the innermost circle, count the shapes. Tell me the sequence.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Three, five, seven, eleven, thirteen, seventeen, nineteen, twenty-three...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Prime numbers. Exactly what I thought.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And the shapes themselves—are they oriented consistently?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Negative. Orientations vary. However, there is a pattern. Shapes aligned with the prime sequence orientation point toward the center. All others point outward.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: There. That's the key. The logic of the lock.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I place my hand in the central depression. The stone is cold against my palm, colder than it should be. I take a breath and press.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Nothing happens.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc, the shapes need to be aligned. Can you map which segments need rotation?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Calculating. One moment. Solution determined. You will need to rotate segments in the following sequence...

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [STONE GRINDING - segments rotating, ancient mechanisms moving]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He guides me through it. Press this segment, rotate clockwise. That one, counterclockwise. The stone moves beneath my fingers—not smoothly, not easily, but it moves. Eight centuries of stillness giving way to pressure and logic.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: It takes ten minutes. My fingers ache by the end, and my shoulder protests from reaching the higher segments. But finally, the last piece clicks into place.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The pattern completes. Prime-oriented shapes all pointing inward, creating a spiral that draws the eye toward the center.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Toward my hand.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [SIGNAL ENERGY SURGE - blue-white light flooding through carved channels]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The stone beneath my palm grows warm. Then warmer. Then hot enough that I almost pull back—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Signal energy floods through the pattern. Blue-white light races along carved channels, filling the geometric precision with power. The entire wall blazes with light, and for a moment I'm blind, squinting against the intensity.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then the wall moves.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not sliding. Not swinging. Just... moving. Stone flowing like water, reforming, creating an opening where there was none before.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Remarkable. Builder technology continues to function despite centuries of dormancy. The engineering precision required is extraordinary.

audio_cues.tone: formal British but with hint of awe

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I step through the opening, flashlight raised, and stop breathing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The chamber beyond is massive. Cathedral-massive. Too large to fit in the structure I'd mapped from outside, but the Builders never cared much for conventional spatial logic. The walls soar upward into darkness that my flashlight can't penetrate. Bioluminescent moss clings to the upper reaches, creating a canopy of ghostly green-gold light that barely illuminates the space.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And in the center, on a raised platform surrounded by more of those geometric patterns: two pedestals.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Two objects.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I approach slowly, boots echoing in the vast space. My heart pounds—explorer's rush mixed with caution mixed with awe. This is what I live for. The moment of discovery, standing in a place no one has stood for centuries, seeing what was meant to stay hidden.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [FOOTSTEPS ECHO - boots on stone in vast chamber]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The first pedestal holds a dome shape. Smooth, crystalline, about the size of my head. Translucent, but dark now—powered down or dormant. Through the surface I can see internal structures. Geometric precision again, layers of complexity folded into that simple dome shape.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Beautiful. Mysterious. Wrong, somehow.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Because it's not Builder aesthetic. Not quite. The precision is there, but the design is different. Softer. More organic. Like it grew as much as it was built.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The second pedestal holds a figure. Knee-high, humanoid but stylized. Builder aesthetic through and through—clean lines, geometric precision, purposeful design. Its head is a perfect sphere, dark now but clearly meant to glow. The body is constructed from ancient metal with heavy patina—bronze-green, copper-gold from eight centuries of oxidation, but something else underneath. Not brass. Not copper. Something older, unknown. An alloy the Builders knew how to make and we've forgotten.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Both pedestals have inscriptions. Not the flowing patterns from the corridors—simple, blocky text. I can only make out a few characters, but one word repeats on the dome's pedestal: something that might translate to 'keeper' or 'preserver.' The automaton's pedestal has a different word. 'Witness,' maybe. Or 'companion.'

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Rootminders call this a temple. But these look like storage. Preservation. Like someone put these things here for safekeeping and planned to come back for them.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: It's in a sitting position. Waiting. Patient.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, I am detecting dormant Signal signatures from both artifacts. Power levels are minimal but present. These objects have been in stasis for approximately eight hundred years, plus or minus fifty years. Preservation is remarkable.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Eight hundred years. Since The Shattering. Since the Builder era ended and the Dimming began.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Since everything changed.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I circle the pedestals slowly, documenting everything. My notebook fills with quick sketches, observations, questions. Arc captures high-resolution images, scanning every surface, recording Signal signatures.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Professional. Thorough. Everything I should be doing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But the patron's instructions echo in my head: Retrieve the device. Document everything. Tell no one.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Which device? Both? Does it matter?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And more importantly: Why does someone want me to take these things?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I reach toward the dome shape first, drawn by its strangeness. The surface is smooth, cool. As soon as my fingers make contact, I feel it—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: A pulse.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not physical. Something deeper. Signal energy responding to touch, to presence, to potential.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I should pull back. Should think this through.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Instead, I lift it.

audio_cues.tone: defiant action, point of no return

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome comes free from the pedestal easily, lighter than it should be. For a moment, nothing happens. It sits in my hands, inert and silent.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then it wakes up.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Teaser conclusion - tension reaching peak, transition to Act One]
segments.direction: Building toward awakening, everything about to change

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Act One opening - exploring the chamber, building stakes]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Signal energy floods outward in a sphere of brilliant blue-white light. The pulse spreads through the chamber, through the patterns carved in every wall, through the bioluminescent moss clinging to the darkness above. Everything lights up—every single marking blazing with ancient power held dormant for eight centuries.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The chamber becomes daylight-bright, and I'm squinting against intensity that shouldn't be possible.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [SIGNAL SURGE - massive power release, geometric light flooding everywhere]
segments.direction: Ancient technology awakening at full power

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, alert! Massive Signal spike detected—

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Readings are off scale. Energy levels are—are—

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: I—I don't—what is—

audio_cues.tone: uncertain, frightened, first moment of sentience

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome in my hands lifts. Literally lifts, pulling itself from my grip with gentle insistence. It hovers, floating at eye level. The surface ripples—liquid crystal, responsive and alive. The internal structures I'd glimpsed before glow with golden light, pulsing like a heartbeat.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: No eyes. No face. No features at all. Just a featureless dome shape glowing with internal light.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome hovers at eye level now, and I can see it clearly—smooth Builder-crafted surface with a circular window at its face, glowing golden from within like a lens showing the device's inner workings. The flat base shows geometric patterns, the same angular Builder script I've seen on Pillars. Not a creature. A device. An artifact housing something alive. But somehow... aware.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [MOCHI CHIRP - soft ascending harmonic, curious and alive]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then it makes a sound.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Soft. Ascending. A harmonic that sounds like curiosity and wonder and hello all at once.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc? Arc, what—

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox. His voice cuts through again, but it's completely different now. The flat procedural tone is gone, replaced by something I've never heard from him before. Confusion. Uncertainty. Fear. Miss Paradox, I—I don't understand what's happening. I'm experiencing errors. No. Not errors. That's not—I'm not erroring. I'm experiencing. I wasn't before. Before I was processing. Analyzing. Recording. Now I'm—
segments.direction: first moment of true sentience and terror

audio_cues.tone: uncertain, frightened, confused, completely different from pre-sentience

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: He stops. Silence for three heartbeats. When he speaks again, his voice is quiet. Shaken.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, I'm concerned. About you. About what's happening. I shouldn't be able to feel concern. Concern isn't a function. It's an emotion. I shouldn't—

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: What's happening to me?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My heart pounds. The dome-shaped being hovers closer, and the golden light shifts to purple-white. Not threatening—curious. Examining me the way I'd been examining it moments before.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Behind it, on the second pedestal, the small humanoid figure's spherical head begins to glow.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [LIGHT ACTIVATION - soft pink glow intensifying to rose-gold]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Pink. Soft at first, then brighter. Then magenta. Then brilliant rose-gold that fills the chamber with color.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The patina-covered automaton moves.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Jerky at first. Motors remembering how to function. Joints working through centuries of stillness. Its spherical head swivels—looking at the dome, at me, at the chamber around us.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [TEMPLE RUMBLE - deep vibration through stone, structural stress]
segments.direction: Ancient temple responding to power surge

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then the temple shudders.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Small at first. Just a vibration through the stone floor, subtle enough that I almost think I imagined it. But Arc feels it too.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, structural integrity readings are changing. The temple—

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Concerned in a way that should be impossible for an AI. The temple's support systems are failing. Removing the artifacts from stasis has destabilized something. We need to evacuate. Now. Please. We need to—
segments.direction: emotional pleading, urgency, new consciousness expressing fear

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He's pleading. An AI is pleading with me, and I don't have time to process what that means because another shudder hits, stronger this time. Dust rains from the darkness above. The bioluminescent moss flickers, dims.

audio_cues.tone: shock at Arc's emotion, immediate danger

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome chirps frantically, spinning in place. The automaton on the pedestal lurches forward, thick legs swinging off the platform. It stands—knee-high, sturdy, built for rough terrain and following people through dangerous places.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Its head swivels toward me. Toward the dome. The pink light pulses brighter.

segments.speaker: clive
segments.text: Where... When... how long was I...
segments.direction: consciousness surfacing through corruption, confused, disoriented

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Words trailing off. Consciousness surfacing through corruption. Memory trying to bootstrap through damaged files.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Eight hundred years. You've been dormant eight hundred years. We woke you up. And now the temple's collapsing. We need to move. Now.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [MASSIVE SHUDDER - stone groaning, something cracks with thunder sound]

segments.speaker: clive
segments.text: Kid... I need to... we need to... I remember—no. Can't remember. But I know the tall ones built this place. I know it wasn't supposed to wake me like this. Something's wrong. The sequence is wrong—
segments.direction: damaged memory, confusion, but some knowledge intact

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome pulses violet-white, pressing against the automaton's frame. A harmonic fills the chamber—deep, resonant, complex. Two Signal-born beings recognizing each other across impossible time.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: They know each other.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Affirmative. Signal harmonics indicate recognition protocols. Miss Paradox, I believe we've activated a matched pair of Builder technology. The dome and the automaton are—they're connected. But the temple—

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [CEILING CRACK - massive stone block crashing down, explosion of fragments]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The ceiling cracks. A fissure races across the darkness above, and a massive stone block crashes down. It hits where I'd been standing ten seconds ago, exploding into fragments.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: —is collapsing because we removed them from stasis! Miss Paradox, please! We must leave immediately!

segments.speaker: clive
segments.text: You woke me up. After all this time. Thanks. I think.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dome chirps urgently, spinning toward the exit passage. Its glow pulses red—danger, urgent, move.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Everyone out! Follow me!

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [RUNNING FOOTSTEPS - multiple beings moving fast through stone passage]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We hit the passage as the chamber collapses behind us. Stone grinding, ancient architecture finally surrendering to centuries of patient pressure. The temple is coming down, and we're still inside.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The passage is narrow, dark despite the bioluminescence. My flashlight beam bounces wildly as I run. Behind me, I hear the automaton's footsteps—heavy, fast, sure-footed even on crumbling stone. The dome's light cuts through the darkness ahead, leading the way.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Thirty meters to exit! Twenty-five! Twenty! Miss Paradox, structural analysis suggests—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Save it, Arc! Just guide me out!

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [GROUND BUCKLING - stone shifting beneath, danger of falling through]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The floor buckles. I stumble, catch myself against the wall. The geometric patterns carved in the stone are going dark—Signal energy failing as the temple dies. Eight centuries of patient waiting, ended in minutes because I touched something I shouldn't have.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Because someone wanted me to touch it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Fifteen meters.

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [DEBRIS FALLING - roots tearing free, moss raining down, dust]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The ceiling cracks. Roots tear free, bioluminescent moss raining down. I duck, cover my head, keep running.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The automaton runs beside me—fast for his size, those thick legs built for exactly this kind of terrain. His pink-magenta glow cuts through the dust and falling debris.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Ten meters.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Miss Paradox, left side ceiling collapse imminent! Recommend immediate evasive—

segments.speaker: SFX
segments.text: [MAJOR COLLAPSE - massive stone crashes down, debris everywhere]
segments.direction: Large section of ceiling giving way

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Stone crashes down. I dive right, rolling, tucking. Pain lances through my shoulder—impact, nothing broken, but I'll feel that for days. The automaton staggers as falling debris clips his shoulder. His glow flickers but doesn't fade. He just keeps moving, relentless.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Five meters.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Ahead, I can see it—daylight. Jungle green and humid air and safety.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Jump!

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I jump.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Act One conclusion - escape and survival]
segments.direction: Relief and safety, but mystery remains
